---
title: "PCA"
date: 2023-05-14
# date-modified: 2023-04-05
categories:
  - PCA
image: iris_pca.png
---



Principle component methods are used to summarize and viusalize the information contained in a large multivariate datasets.

Principle component analysis is used to extract the important information from a multivariate data table and to express this information as a set of few new variables called principle components. These new variables correspond to a linear combination of the originals. The number of principal components is less than or equal to the number of original variables.

![](pca.png)

## R packages


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.1     ✔ readr     2.1.4
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.0
## ✔ ggplot2   3.4.2     ✔ tibble    3.2.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.2     ✔ tidyr     1.3.0
## ✔ purrr     1.0.1     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(FactoMineR) # compute principal component methods
library(factoextra) # extract, visualize and interpretate the results
## Welcome! Want to learn more? See two factoextra-related books at https://goo.gl/ve3WBa
```
:::

![](pca2.png)

## PCA for continuous variables


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
data(decathlon2)
head(decathlon2)
##           X100m Long.jump Shot.put High.jump X400m X110m.hurdle Discus
## SEBRLE    11.04      7.58    14.83      2.07 49.81        14.69  43.75
## CLAY      10.76      7.40    14.26      1.86 49.37        14.05  50.72
## BERNARD   11.02      7.23    14.25      1.92 48.93        14.99  40.87
## YURKOV    11.34      7.09    15.19      2.10 50.42        15.31  46.26
## ZSIVOCZKY 11.13      7.30    13.48      2.01 48.62        14.17  45.67
## McMULLEN  10.83      7.31    13.76      2.13 49.91        14.38  44.41
##           Pole.vault Javeline X1500m Rank Points Competition
## SEBRLE          5.02    63.19  291.7    1   8217    Decastar
## CLAY            4.92    60.15  301.5    2   8122    Decastar
## BERNARD         5.32    62.77  280.1    4   8067    Decastar
## YURKOV          4.72    63.44  276.4    5   8036    Decastar
## ZSIVOCZKY       4.42    55.37  268.0    7   8004    Decastar
## McMULLEN        4.42    56.37  285.1    8   7995    Decastar
```
:::

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
### subset data
decathlon2_active <- decathlon2[1:23, 1:10]
### standardize data and PCA
res_pca <- PCA(
   decathlon2_active, 
   scale.unit = TRUE,
   graph = FALSE 
)
### extract the eigenvalues/variances of principal components
eig_val <- get_eigenvalue(res_pca)
eig_val
##        eigenvalue variance.percent cumulative.variance.percent
## Dim.1   4.1242133        41.242133                    41.24213
## Dim.2   1.8385309        18.385309                    59.62744
## Dim.3   1.2391403        12.391403                    72.01885
## Dim.4   0.8194402         8.194402                    80.21325
## Dim.5   0.7015528         7.015528                    87.22878
## Dim.6   0.4228828         4.228828                    91.45760
## Dim.7   0.3025817         3.025817                    94.48342
## Dim.8   0.2744700         2.744700                    97.22812
## Dim.9   0.1552169         1.552169                    98.78029
## Dim.10  0.1219710         1.219710                   100.00000

fviz_eig(res_pca, addlabels = TRUE, ylim = c(0, 50))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}

var <- get_pca_var(res_pca)
# Coordinates
head(var$coord)
##                   Dim.1       Dim.2      Dim.3       Dim.4      Dim.5
## X100m        -0.8506257 -0.17939806  0.3015564  0.03357320 -0.1944440
## Long.jump     0.7941806  0.28085695 -0.1905465 -0.11538956  0.2331567
## Shot.put      0.7339127  0.08540412  0.5175978  0.12846837 -0.2488129
## High.jump     0.6100840 -0.46521415  0.3300852  0.14455012  0.4027002
## X400m        -0.7016034  0.29017826  0.2835329  0.43082552  0.1039085
## X110m.hurdle -0.7641252 -0.02474081  0.4488873 -0.01689589  0.2242200
# Cos2: quality on the factore map
head(var$cos2)
##                  Dim.1        Dim.2      Dim.3        Dim.4      Dim.5
## X100m        0.7235641 0.0321836641 0.09093628 0.0011271597 0.03780845
## Long.jump    0.6307229 0.0788806285 0.03630798 0.0133147506 0.05436203
## Shot.put     0.5386279 0.0072938636 0.26790749 0.0165041211 0.06190783
## High.jump    0.3722025 0.2164242070 0.10895622 0.0208947375 0.16216747
## X400m        0.4922473 0.0842034209 0.08039091 0.1856106269 0.01079698
## X110m.hurdle 0.5838873 0.0006121077 0.20149984 0.0002854712 0.05027463
# Contributions to the principal components
head(var$contrib)
##                  Dim.1      Dim.2     Dim.3       Dim.4     Dim.5
## X100m        17.544293  1.7505098  7.338659  0.13755240  5.389252
## Long.jump    15.293168  4.2904162  2.930094  1.62485936  7.748815
## Shot.put     13.060137  0.3967224 21.620432  2.01407269  8.824401
## High.jump     9.024811 11.7715838  8.792888  2.54987951 23.115504
## X400m        11.935544  4.5799296  6.487636 22.65090599  1.539012
## X110m.hurdle 14.157544  0.0332933 16.261261  0.03483735  7.166193
```
:::


### color by groups


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
head(iris, 3)
##   Sepal.Length Sepal.Width Petal.Length Petal.Width Species
## 1          5.1         3.5          1.4         0.2  setosa
## 2          4.9         3.0          1.4         0.2  setosa
## 3          4.7         3.2          1.3         0.2  setosa
iris_pca <- PCA(iris[, -5], graph = FALSE)

fviz_pca_ind(
  iris_pca,
  geom.ind = "point", # show points only
  col.ind = iris$Species, # color by groups
  palette = c("#00AFBB", "#E7B800", "#FC4E07"),
  addEllipses = TRUE, # concentratoin ellipse
  legend.title = "Groups"
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-1.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}

# Add confidence ellipses
fviz_pca_ind(
  iris_pca, 
  geom.ind = "point", 
  col.ind = iris$Species, 
  palette = c("#00AFBB", "#E7B800", "#FC4E07"),
  addEllipses = TRUE, 
  ellipse.type = "confidence",
  legend.title = "Groups"
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-2.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}

fviz_pca_ind(
  iris_pca,
  label = "none", # hide individual labels
  habillage = iris$Species, # color by groups
  addEllipses = TRUE, # Concentration ellipses
  palette = "jco"
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-3.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}

# Variables on dimensions 2 and 3
fviz_pca_var(res_pca, axes = c(2, 3))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-4.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# Individuals on dimensions 2 and 3
fviz_pca_ind(res_pca, axes = c(2, 3))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-4-5.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Show variable points and text labels
fviz_pca_var(res_pca, geom.var = c("point", "text"))
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-5-1.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}

# Show individuals text labels only
fviz_pca_ind(res_pca, geom.ind =  "text")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-5-2.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}

# Change the size of arrows an labels
fviz_pca_var(
  res_pca, 
  arrowsize = 1, 
  labelsize = 5, 
  repel = TRUE
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-5-3.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}

# Change points size, shape and fill color
# Change labelsize
fviz_pca_ind(
  res_pca, 
  pointsize = 3, 
  pointshape = 21, 
  fill = "lightblue",
  labelsize = 5, 
  repel = TRUE
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-5-4.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
# Add confidence ellipses
fviz_pca_ind(
  iris_pca, 
  geom.ind = "point", 
  col.ind = iris$Species, # color by groups
  palette = c("#00AFBB", "#E7B800", "#FC4E07"),
  addEllipses = TRUE, 
  ellipse.type = "confidence",
  legend.title = "Groups"
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-6-1.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}

# Convex hull
fviz_pca_ind(
  iris_pca, 
  geom.ind = "point",
  col.ind = iris$Species, # color by groups
  palette = c("#00AFBB", "#E7B800", "#FC4E07"),
  addEllipses = TRUE, 
  ellipse.type = "convex",
  legend.title = "Groups"
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-6-2.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
fviz_pca_ind(
  iris_pca,
  geom.ind = "point", # show points only (but not "text")
  group.ind = iris$Species, # color by groups
  legend.title = "Groups",
  mean.point = FALSE
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-7-1.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ind.p <- fviz_pca_ind(
  iris_pca, 
  geom = "point", 
  col.ind = iris$Species
)

ggpubr::ggpar(
  ind.p,
  title = "Principal Component Analysis",
  subtitle = "Iris data set",
  caption = "Source: factoextra",
  xlab = "PC1", ylab = "PC2",
  legend.title = "Species", 
  legend.position = "top",
  ggtheme = theme_gray(), palette = "jco"
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-8-1.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
fviz_pca_biplot(res_pca, repel = TRUE,
                col.var = "#2E9FDF", # Variables color
                col.ind = "#696969"  # Individuals color
                )
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-9-1.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}

fviz_pca_biplot(
  iris_pca, 
  col.ind = iris$Species, 
  palette = "jco", 
  addEllipses = TRUE, 
  label = "var",
  col.var = "black", 
  repel = TRUE,
  legend.title = "Species"
) 
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-9-2.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
fviz_pca_biplot(
  iris_pca, 
  # Fill individuals by groups
  geom.ind = "point",
  pointshape = 21,
  pointsize = 2.5,
  fill.ind = iris$Species,
  col.ind = "black",
  # Color variable by groups
  col.var = factor(c("sepal", "sepal", "petal", "petal")),
  
  legend.title = list(fill = "Species", color = "Clusters"),
  repel = TRUE    # Avoid label overplotting
)+
  ggpubr::fill_palette("jco")+      # Indiviual fill color
  ggpubr::color_palette("npg")      # Variable colors
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-10-1.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
fviz_pca_biplot(
  iris_pca, 
  # Individuals
  geom.ind = "point",
  fill.ind = iris$Species, 
  col.ind = "black",
  pointshape = 21, 
  pointsize = 2,
  palette = "jco",
  addEllipses = TRUE,
  # Variables
  alpha.var ="contrib", 
  col.var = "contrib",
  gradient.cols = "RdYlBu",
  legend.title = list(
    fill = "Species", 
    color = "Contrib",
    alpha = "Contrib"
    )
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](index_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-11-1.png){fig-align='center' width=672}
:::
:::


## Reference

1. [Principal Component Methods in R: Practical Guide](http://www.sthda.com/english/articles/31-principal-component-methods-in-r-practical-guide/)
2. [Principal Component Analysis in R: prcomp vs princomp](http://www.sthda.com/english/articles/31-principal-component-methods-in-r-practical-guide/118-principal-component-analysis-in-r-prcomp-vs-princomp/)
